parentQuestion: Which of the following statements is FALSE? "Damage to the primary (somatic) mot...

scaffolds.q: Voluntary movements (like reaching for a cup) are controlled by the brain's motor cortex.
scaffolds.explain: The primary motor cortex in the frontal lobe initiates voluntary movements. Neurons there send signals down the spinal cord to activate muscles. Lose the cortex, lose voluntary control.

scaffolds.q: A reflex (like the knee-jerk response) requires the brain to process the information.
scaffolds.explain: Reflexes are processed at the spinal cord level — the signal goes to the spinal cord and back to the muscle without needing brain involvement. This is why reflexes are so fast. The spinal cord is the local controller; the brain is just an observer.
